author: David
text: Converting a J40 to hydraulic power steering is not something you can realistically do from home with no tools. It’s a mechanical fabrication job involving cutting, drilling, and installing major steering components. The safest approach is either buying a full bolt-on kit or having a workshop do the fabrication. I’ll explain what the minimum realistic setup looks like. What a hydraulic conversion actually requires A J40 was designed with a manual steering box and bell-crank system, so power steering requires installing a completely different steering box and linkage. Core parts required: Steering components Power steering steering box (often from Land Cruiser 60/70 or Toyota Hilux) Pitman arm Drag link Modified steering shaft with U-joints Hydraulic system Power steering pump Pump mounting bracket Reservoir High-pressure hose Return hose Engine drive Double crank pulley Drive belt Frame mounting Steering box mounting plate Reinforcement plate Tools normally required At minimum: Basic tools socket set spanners jack + axle stands breaker bar Fabrication tools drill angle grinder welder (almost always required) Without these, the conversion is essentially impossible. Realistic options if you have no tools Option 1 — Buy a full conversion kit Some kits are designed to minimize fabrication. Example suppliers: Cruiser Corps Man-A-Fre BTB Products Cool Cruisers These typically include: steering box mounting plate pump hoses steering shaft Even with a kit you still need: drilling some cutting A garage can install it in 5–8 hours. Option 2 — Have a 4x4 workshop do it Best option if starting from nothing. Typical cost depending on parts: Parts: $600–1500 Labour: $300–600 In Pakistan the labour is usually much cheaper. Option 3 — Electric power steering column If you truly want minimal tools, EPS is the only realistic DIY approach. Steps are basically: Remove steering column Install EPS column motor unit Wire to battery Connect to steering box But even this usually requires: cutting the steering shaft welding or adapters If you want the easiest possible path The simplest solution for a J40 owner without tools is: Take it to a Land Cruiser specialist and install a Toyota 60-series power steering box conversion. This is the most common J40 upgrade worldwide. 💡 If you want, I can also show you: The easiest J40 power steering conversion ever designed (almost bolt-in) The cheapest conversion using Toyota Hilux parts The mistake that cracks the J40 frame during conversion Those three things save most people a lot of money and frustration.

author: David
text: nd do bolt on kits exist to conver to haudralic? Yes — bolt-on kits do exist for converting a Land Cruiser J40 to hydraulic power steering, but there’s an important catch: 👉 Almost none are truly 100% bolt-on. They are usually “conversion kits” that reduce fabrication, but most still require some welding or drilling. Example hydraulic conversion kits for the J40 Complete Saginaw power steering kit A typical example is a Saginaw conversion kit like the one from Cruiser Solutions. It normally includes: power steering gearbox steering shaft pump pump bracket mounting plate pitman arm drag link high and low pressure hoses pulley and belt system These kits run roughly $1,100–$1,600 depending on completeness. Installation reality Even with a kit: Typical work required: Weld steering box mounting plate to frame Drill or cut hole in front crossmember Modify steering column Install pump bracket and pulley The steering box mount must be welded because it carries very high steering forces, and installers recommend a certified welder. So the kits are “semi-bolt-on”, not truly plug-and-play. Types of kits you’ll see There are basically three J40 conversion styles. 1️⃣ Saginaw steering conversion Most common worldwide. Uses: GM steering box GM pump Pros: very strong easy parts availability Cons: most fabrication 2️⃣ Toyota mini-truck box conversion Uses steering box from: 1979–1985 Toyota pickup / Hilux Pros: more compact more “Toyota” Cons: still some welding 3️⃣ FJ60 steering box conversion Uses steering from the 60-series Land Cruiser. Pros: extremely robust good steering feel popular with big tyres Cons: slightly more complex install The closest thing to “bolt-on” There are kits that provide all mounting plates and shafts, so you just install the steering box and pump. But even those still require: welding one mount drilling a crossmember There’s no true IKEA-style bolt-on hydraulic kit because the original J40 chassis was never designed for power steering. The real easiest path (most owners do this) Instead of buying a full kit: Most Land Cruiser builders simply install: **Toyota Hilux steering box Saginaw pump custom mount plate** This is cheaper and extremely reliable. ✅ Summary Option Difficulty True bolt-on hydraulic kit ❌ doesn’t really exist Conversion kit ⚠️ some welding required Custom Hilux/FJ60 swap ⚠️ moderate fabrication Electric power steering ✅ easiest install 💡 If you want, I can also show you: The easiest J40 power steering conversion ever done (very clever design) The Hilux parts list that makes the conversion extremely cheap The steering upgrade that makes a J40 drive like a modern 4×4 Those three things are what most serious Land Cruiser builders end up doing.

author: David
text: Good — if you want to completely re-wire a 1978 Toyota Land Cruiser J40, I’ll give you three things: The exact Daraz shopping list (everything needed) The ideal modern wiring architecture for a J40 The fuse box layout + wiring plan This is essentially the same method professional restorers use for old Land Cruisers. 1️⃣ The Core Re-Wiring Kit (Buy First) Universal 21‑Circuit Automotive Wiring Harness Kit Price not available • Go to merchant Universal Extra‑Long 21‑Circuit Car Wiring Harness Price not available • Go to merchant 21‑Circuit Hot Rod Wiring Harness Kit Price not available • Go to merchant Universal 21‑Circuit Vehicle Electrical Wiring Kit Price not available • Go to merchant A 21-circuit harness kit is ideal for a classic 4x4. Typical circuits included: headlights brake lights ignition coil horn turn signals gauges wipers radio dome light backup lights fuel pump accessories These harnesses are colour-coded and labelled to simplify installation. 2️⃣ Everything Else You Must Buy (Daraz List) Search these exact items on Daraz. Automotive Wire Kit Buy stranded copper automotive wire. Recommended gauges: Gauge Use 4 AWG battery cables 10 AWG alternator / main power 12 AWG headlights 14 AWG ignition 16 AWG accessories 18 AWG gauges Automotive wiring uses the AWG system where lower numbers mean thicker wire capable of carrying more current. Search: automotive primary wire kit 12v car wire roll stranded copper automotive wire Connectors Kit Search: automotive electrical connector kit crimp terminal kit ring terminal assortment bullet connector kit You will need: ring terminals spade connectors bullet connectors heat shrink solder sleeves Wire Protection Search: split wire loom tubing braided cable sleeve rubber firewall grommets Sizes: 6mm 10mm 16mm 25mm Fuse and Relay System Search: 12 way blade fuse box 12v relay with socket relay block automotive Minimum relays: headlights horn auxiliary lights radiator fan accessories Battery and Starter Cables Search: 4 gauge battery cable kit heavy duty battery terminals starter cable automotive Ground Straps Classic Land Cruisers rely heavily on chassis ground. Search: braided ground strap engine ground cable Minimum grounds: battery → chassis engine → frame body → frame Lighting Connectors Search: H4 headlight connector indicator bulb socket tail light socket Tools (if doing it yourself) Search: ratcheting crimp tool wire stripper heat gun multimeter soldering iron 3️⃣ Ideal Modern Wiring Architecture for a J40 A good wiring system splits the truck into four harness sections. 1️⃣ Engine Harness Runs to: starter alternator ignition coil temperature sender oil pressure sender electric fan 2️⃣ Front Lighting Harness Runs to: headlights indicators parking lights horn 3️⃣ Dash Harness Runs to: ignition switch gauges heater fan wipers radio switches 4️⃣ Rear Harness Runs to: tail lights brake lights reverse lights fuel sender number plate light A wiring harness bundles these cables together so they are protected from vibration, abrasion, and moisture. 4️⃣ Ideal Fuse Box Layout Mount the fuse box under the dashboard. Suggested layout: Fuse Circuit 1 ignition 2 headlights 3 tail lights 4 indicators 5 horn 6 wipers 7 heater 8 gauges 9 radio 10 reverse lights 11 accessories 12 spare 5️⃣ Headlight Relay Upgrade (Important) Old Land Cruisers ran headlights directly through the switch. Upgrade: battery ↓ relay ↓ headlights Benefits: brighter lights longer switch life less voltage drop 6️⃣ Where to Route Wires in a J40 Typical routing: Engine bay → firewall grommet → fuse box under dash Rear harness: Dash → along driver side floor → rear lights 7️⃣ Estimated Cost in Pakistan Item PKR Harness kit 20k – 40k Wire 6k Connectors 4k Fuse / relay 5k Loom 3k Tools 8k Total: 45k – 70k PKR 8️⃣ Pro Tips for a J40 Rewire Do these while you're at it: upgrade headlights to H4 add USB charger add aux fuse block add winch wiring add LED light bar relay This makes the truck far more usable. ✅ If you want, I can

author: David
text: What this kit already includes A universal wiring harness like this normally includes: pre-wired 12-circuit fuse block labelled wires for each circuit horn relay headlight wiring turn signal wiring alternator plug ignition switch connectors hazard flasher headlight dimmer connector These kits are designed for classic cars, hot rods, trucks, and custom vehicles and cover the main circuits such as ignition, lights, gauges, horn, wipers, etc. Why this is ideal for your 1978 J40 The FJ40 electrical system is simple and fits comfortably within 10–14 circuits, including: ignition headlights brake lights tail lights indicators horn wipers gauges heater fan radio reverse lights So a 12-circuit harness usually covers everything needed for the truck. What you still add to the cart Add these extra items alongside the harness: 4 AWG battery cable (starter cable) 6 AWG cable (alternator charge wire) automotive connector kit split loom tubing Those are cheap and complete the install.

text: just wondering. what would a full-spring suspension conversion cost?

author: David
text: hmm. erm A full coil / 4-link suspension conversion on a J40 is not a kit job — it’s basically re-engineering the entire underside of the vehicle. 💰 Real-world cost breakdown 1. Parts (bare minimum) Even DIY parts add up: 4-link kit components: $600–$1,000 Coilovers (good ones): $800–$2,000+ Brackets, mounts, joints: $500–$1,500 Steering corrections (high steer, links): $800–$1,500 👉 Parts total: $3,000 – $6,000 2. Fabrication & labour (the big one) This is where costs explode. A proper conversion involves: cutting off all leaf spring mounts welding new link mounts to frame + axle building 4-link geometry setting pinion angles relocating shocks often modifying the chassis Typical fabrication time: 80–150+ hours Workshop cost (depending where): In Pakistan (good fabricator) PKR 300k – 1,000k (~$1,000 – $3,500) International / high-end shops $5,000 – $15,000+ 3. Hidden costs (people underestimate these) driveshaft modification / custom shafts brake line rerouting exhaust rework fuel tank relocation (sometimes) bump stops / limit straps 👉 Add another $500 – $2,000 💸 Total realistic cost Budget DIY + local fabrication (Pakistan) 👉 PKR 500k – 1.5M (~$1,800 – $5,500) Proper engineered build 👉 $6,000 – $12,000 High-end (ICON-style build) 👉 $15,000 – $30,000+
